title: Ying Nan

{{Quote|You are a product of all who came before you. The legacy of your family, the good and the bad, it is all a part of who you are. Stop hiding, nephew. It only prolongs the pain.|Ying Nan to [[Shang-Chi]]|Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings}}
'''Ying Nan''' (Mandarin: '''應南''') is a guardian of [[Ta Lo]], the sister of [[Ying Li]], and the aunt of [[Shang-Chi]] and [[Xu Xialing]].

==Biography==
{{MD|Ying Nan (Earth-199999)}}
===Resident of Ta Lo===
{{Quote|Our people have been here for over four thousand years, preparing for something we hope will never happen. We are the keepers of the [[Dark Gate]], sworn to protect this [[Ta Lo|realm]] and [[Earth|yours]] from the [[Dweller-in-Darkness|evil]] that’s locked behind it.|Ying Nan|Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings}}
As an inhabitant of the legendary village of [[Ta Lo]], Ying Nan was dedicated to the protection of the [[Earth|Earthly Plane]] from the [[Soul Eaters]] that lied beyond the [[Dark Gate]].

Ying became separated from her sister [[Ying Li]] after she married [[Xu Wenwu]] and the elders of Ta Lo refused to welcome the latter within the village. She was saddened upon learning of her sister's death, and had a memorial set up in the village to honor her memory.{{Ref|SCatLotTR}}

===Meeting Niece and Nephew===
{{Quote|After losing someone like [[Ying Li|her]], it's easy to feel alone in this world. But you're not. You are children of this family, and I welcome you [[Ta Lo|home]].|Ying Nan to [[Shang-Chi]] and [[Xu Xialing]]|Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings}}
Ying was among the crowd of residents of [[Ta Lo]] when her nephew [[Shang-Chi]] and her niece [[Xu Xialing]] made their way to the village, guided by [[Morris]]. Unlike [[Guang Bo]], who was wary of these outsiders, Ying warmly welcomed the group in the village, even hugging Shang-Chi's friend [[Katy Chen]] as she introduced herself. Upon learning that [[Xu Wenwu]] was coming to Ta Lo in an attempt to breach the [[Dark Gate]], as he believed his late wife [[Ying Li]] to be locked behind, Ying proceeded to narrate the legend of Ta Lo, and how its inhabitants protected the world from the [[Dweller-in-Darkness]] and his [[Soul Eaters]], who were feeding Wenwu lies about his wife in order to bring the gates down and invade the realm of Ta Lo and everything that lied beyond.

Ying then took her niece and nephew to the memorial in honor of their mother, before giving them traditional clothes of Ta Lo and formally welcoming them into the village. Later, as she noticed Xialing watching young girl train for battle without taking part herself, Ying reminded her niece that unlike what she had known among the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]], men and women of Ta Lo were viewed as equals regarding what they could do. She then gave Xialing a [[Xu Xialing's Rope Dart|rope dart]] she could train for the impending [[Battle of Ta Lo|battle]].

Ying then left to train on her own in a small arena, where she was approached by her nephew, who asked her to teach him the fighting style of the protectors of Ta Lo, since he knew that it had enabled his mother to beat Wenwu. Ying agreed to train Shang-Chi, accompanying his progression and noticing that he seemed blocked because he did not fully embraced his own heritage. Ying thus motivated her nephew to accept who he was an to take the best in both his mother's and father's legacy.{{Ref|SCatLotTR}}

===[[Battle of Ta Lo]]===
{{Quote|I miss my [[Ying Li|sister]] as much as you do, but this is not the way to honor her memory.|Ying Nan to [[Xu Wenwu]]|Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings}}
Ying stood in the frontline when the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] led by [[Xu Wenwu]] made their way to [[Ta Lo]] in an attempt to invade it and breach the [[Dark Gate]]. She attempted to reason with Wenwu, stating that the voice of [[Ying Li]] he was hearing actually came from the [[Dweller-in-Darkness]], but her words fell on deaf ear as Wenwu remained persuaded that his wife was held captive by the elders of [[Ta Lo]]. Ying thus had no choice but to fight against the Ten Rings.

Disposing of her initial opponents with ease thanks to her mastery with her [[Dragon Scale Staff]], Ying went to confront Wenwu, but instead found herself forced to fight against the [[Death Dealer]], against whom she was able to hold her own. As the battle seemed to get in favor of the Ten Rings, Ying noticed that Wenwu had used his [[Ten Rings|rings]] to breach the Dark Gate, causing the arrival of [[Soul Eaters]]. Ying warned [[Razor Fist]] that the defenders of Ta Lo and the Ten Rings had to ally to fight this new enemy, and although Razor Fist initially dismissed her offer, he was convinced after a Soul Eater killed the Death Dealer and Ying saved Razor Fist himself from another creature.

Ying joined the troops gathered on the shore of the lake of Ta Lo to fight off the swarm of Soul Eaters that had been released from the Dark Gate. She then watched in awe as [[Shang-Chi]] emerged from the lake, riding the [[Great Protector]]. She instructed her nephew to find and stop his father while she continued battling the Soul Eaters, eventually witnessing the arrival of the Dweller-in-Darkness. However, the creature was eventually defeated by Shang-Chi, and Ying survived the battle.

In the aftermath of the battle, Ying conducted a ceremony to honor the memory of all those who had fallen to the Soul Eaters.{{Ref|SCatLotTR}}

==Powers and Abilities==
===Powers===
*'''[[Great Protector]] Empowerment''': As a Guardian of Ta Lo, Ying is empowered by the [[Great Protector]]'s magic so that she can protect Ta Lo from [[Dweller-in-Darkness|threats]], gaining a range of abilities such as enhanced strength, durability, stamina, aerokinesis, and a higher control over one's energy. 
**'''Superhuman Strength''': The magic of the Great Protector gives Ying some degree of superior strength. As such, when training her nephew [[Shang-Chi]], she was able to strongly hit him to demonstrate her powers.
**'''Superhuman Durability''': She possesses heightened resistance to physical damage due to mystical empowerment.  
**'''Superhuman Speed''': Her movement and combat speed is enhanced beyond normal human capability.  
**'''Superhuman Agility''': With the empowered magic, Ying's agility has exceptional balance, flexibility, coordination, and aerial maneuverability.  
**'''Superhuman Stamina''': The aerokinetic magic enhanced her stamina allowing her to fight and exert herself for extended periods without fatigue as demonstrated when battling against the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] including the [[Soul Eaters]].
**'''Superhuman Reflexes''': Ying is able to swiftly react to an opponent's attack, blocking their assaults while performing her own moves in retaliation.
**'''Air Manipulation''': The most notable feature of the Great Protector's empowerment was an aerokinesis ability that Ying perfectly controlled, being able to manipulate streams of air around her and use them for offensive and defensive purposes.
***'''Air Blasts''': Ying was able to generate direct concussive blasts made of pure air which could strongly hit her opponents.
***'''Hurricane Generation''': By taking control of small air currents, Ying was able to generate small tornadoes that were powerful enough to carry human opponents away, causing them to fall on the ground.

===Abilities===
*'''Master Martial Artist''': Ying was a tremendously capable martial artist, being one of the most seasoned warriors of [[Ta Lo]]. As such, she was able to train her nephew [[Shang-Chi]], being perfectly able to hold her own against him despite the heavy training he had previously received.
*'''Staff Mastery''': Ying was a proficient user of the [[Dragon Scale Staff]], which she notably used against both the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] and the [[Soul Eaters]] during the [[Battle of Ta Lo]].
*'''Master Acrobat''': Combining her fighting skills and her control over the [[Great Protector]]'s magic, Ying was able to perform skills of incredible agility, moving and jumping around her opponents with ease.
*'''Bilingualism''': Ying is fluent in her native Mandarin, as well as English.

==Equipment==
===Weapons===
*'''[[Dragon Scale Staff]]''': Ying used a fighting staff as her weapon of choice. During the [[Battle of Ta Lo]], she was able to take down many soldiers of the [[Ten Rings (Organization)|Ten Rings]] with her staff, even holding her own against the [[Death Dealer]]. Later in battle, she used the extremity of her staff, which was made of dragon scale, to kill [[Soul Eaters]].

==Trivia==
*In Marvel's official cast announcement in December 2020, [[Michelle Yeoh]] and [[Fala Chen]]'s characters were named Jiang Nan and Jiang Li respectively.<ref>[https://www.marvel.com/articles/movies/shang-chi-legend-ten-rings-cast ‘Shang-Chi and The Legend of The Ten Rings’ Reveals Additional Cast]</ref> The "Jiang" family name was also featured in merchandise for Ying Li.<ref>[https://www.shopdisney.co.uk/funko-jiang-li-pop-vinyl-figure-shang-chi-and-the-legend-of-the-ten-rings-461011913930.html Funko Jiang Li Pop! Vinyl Figure, Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings]</ref>

==Behind the Scenes==
*[[Shao-Yan Li]] was a stunt double for [[Michelle Yeoh]] in the role of Ying Nan.
